skill.name: research-paper-downloader
skill.description: Download academic papers from open-access sources when the user provides a DOI/arXiv ID or requests a keyword-based paper search, and return the saved PDF path.
skill.summary: Automatically find and download open-access academic papers by DOI, arXiv ID, or keyword search.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 167 potential issues across 6 files (1214 lines). All findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. Network requests target legitimate academic APIs (Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Unpaywall, arXiv, PubMed, Crossref). External command patterns exist only in markdown documentation code blocks, not in executed code. Cryptographic algorithm flags are from markdown text and JSON data files, not executable code. The skill includes proper security controls: path traversal protection via validate_output_path, PDF content validation using magic bytes, configurable request timeouts, input sanitization for DOI and arXiv IDs, and no credential handling. No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected. Heuristic combination findings (code execution + network + credential access) are the sum of individual false positives and do not represent actual risk.

content.user_title: Download Open-Access Academic Papers by DOI or Keyword
content.value_statement: Finding and downloading open-access academic papers usually requires checking multiple databases manually. This skill automatically searches across Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Unpaywall, arXiv, PubMed, and Crossref to find and download the PDF you need by DOI, arXiv ID, or keyword search.
actual_capabilities: Download papers by DOI with automatic multi-source fallback across 6 academic databases
Download papers by arXiv ID with alternate mirror support
Search papers by keywords and batch download open-access results
Validate PDF content using magic bytes before saving
Enforce path restrictions so files are saved only inside the configured output directory
Apply configurable request timeouts and retry logic for transient network failures
limitations: Only works with open-access papers that have a freely available PDF
Cannot bypass paywalls, subscription-content, or restricted-access articles
Some academic APIs impose rate limits (100 requests per 5 minutes on free tiers)
Requires a working internet connection to access remote APIs

use_cases.title: Literature Review for Researchers
use_cases.description: A researcher collecting papers for a literature review provides DOIs from their reference manager and receives the corresponding PDF files automatically.

use_cases.title: Course Reading Material for Students
use_cases.description: A student downloads open-access papers for a class assignment by searching keywords related to their topic and setting a limit on how many papers to retrieve.

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Data Collection
use_cases.description: A meta-analyst working on a systematic review batch downloads papers from a curated list of DOIs and arXiv IDs, with each PDF saved under a consistent naming convention.

prompt_templates.title: Download Paper by DOI
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the paper with DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2 and save it to my Downloads folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a specific DOI and wants the corresponding open-access PDF.

prompt_templates.title: Download Paper by arXiv ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the arXiv paper with ID 1706.03762 and save the PDF to the default output directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has an arXiv preprint identifier and wants the PDF.

prompt_templates.title: Search and Download Papers by Keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for papers about "transformer attention mechanism" and download the top 3 open-access results to ~/Downloads/ResearchPapers.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to discover and download papers on a topic without knowing specific identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Download Multiple Papers
prompt_templates.prompt: Download open-access PDFs for these DOIs: 10.1038/nature12345, 10.1126/science.1058040, and arXiv ID 2310.12345. Save all files to ~/research/papers.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a list of references from multiple sources and wants them all downloaded at once.

output_examples.input: Search for papers about "deep learning in radiology" and download the top 2 results

best_practices: Use the full DOI without prefixes like doi: or URL wrappers for best results
Start searches with specific keywords to get more relevant open-access results
Check the configured output directory before running large batch downloads
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to attempt downloading paywalled or subscription-only papers
Do not set max_results higher than 10 for keyword searches to avoid excessive API calls
Do not modify the allowed_domains list in config.json to add untrusted endpoints

faq.question: Which academic sources does this skill search?
faq.answer: The skill searches Semantic Scholar, Europe PMC, OpenAlex, Unpaywall, arXiv, PubMed, and Crossref in priority order, stopping at the first successful download.

faq.question: Does this skill work with paywalled or subscription papers?
faq.answer: No. The skill only downloads papers that have a freely available open-access PDF. It cannot bypass paywalls or access subscription content.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. All supported academic APIs provide free access without authentication. Unpaywall optionally accepts an email parameter for better results.

faq.question: Where are downloaded papers saved?
faq.answer: Files are saved to the configured output directory in config.json. The default is ~/Downloads/ResearchPapers. Path restrictions ensure files cannot be written outside this directory.

faq.question: What happens if a paper is not available as open access?
faq.answer: The skill tries all configured sources and provides a clear failure message listing which sources were attempted. It then suggests accessing the paper directly through the DOI link.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill only makes network requests to academic APIs and writes PDF files to disk. No shell commands or external code execution occurs.
